items.headline: Condo rules cannot stop you from renting out your unit, BCA confirms
items.summary: The Building and Construction Authority has ruled that condo management bodies cannot use estate by-laws to prevent owners from renting out their units. The right to rent belongs to the owner, not the management corporation.
items.meaning: If your condo management has been blocking rentals through estate rules, those rules are not legally enforceable and you are protected as an owner.

items.headline: Condo resale prices climbed in May 2026 even as fewer deals were done
items.summary: Condo resale prices recovered in May 2026, bouncing back from recent softness, even as the number of transactions dipped. Sellers are holding firm on prices despite lower buying activity.
items.meaning: Buyers hoping for resale condo bargains will be disappointed for now, as prices are not falling even when fewer deals are happening.

items.headline: June BTO exercise draws over 22,000 applications, singles pile into one project
items.summary: The June 2026 BTO exercise attracted 22,312 applications across multiple projects, with one project drawing especially heavy interest from singles applying under the Singles scheme.
items.meaning: Competition for new HDB flats remains high, so check the application rates carefully before you commit to a project to gauge your real chances.

items.headline: Around 8,000 BTO flats coming in October 2026, including Bayshore and Toa Payoh
items.summary: HDB plans to launch roughly 8,000 flats in the October 2026 BTO exercise, with projects in Bayshore and Toa Payoh expected to attract the most interest given their locations.
items.meaning: If you are planning a BTO application this year, October will be a significant exercise with some prime locations worth preparing for early.

items.headline: Condo and HDB rents both eased in May 2026 as more supply enters the market
items.summary: Rental prices for both condos and HDB flats dipped in May 2026, continuing a gradual softening as more units become available. The market is described as moving toward a better balance between supply and demand.
items.meaning: If you rent out your property, expect slightly lower rental income than a year ago; if you are a tenant, you now have a little more room to negotiate.

items.headline: Kallang/Whampoa executive HDB flat sells for record $1.41 million
items.summary: A 32-year-old executive flat in the Kallang/Whampoa estate changed hands for $1.412 million, setting a new price record for the area. Its large size, central location, and remaining lease length drove the price.
items.meaning: Million-dollar HDB prices are still being set in central estates, which gives buyers and sellers a concrete benchmark for what similar flats in the area are worth.

items.headline: Government releases two new private housing plots in Bright Hill and Kallang for sale
items.summary: The Urban Redevelopment Authority put two smaller government land sale sites up for tender, one near Sin Ming in the Bright Hill area and one in Kallang. Developers are expected to bid strongly given limited recent supply in those areas.
items.meaning: New private condo projects in Bright Hill and Kallang will eventually follow these land sales, giving buyers in those areas more options in a few years.

items.headline: River Valley Green land sold at $1,730 per square foot of buildable area, pointing to steep future launch prices
items.summary: A developer joint venture paid $1,730 for every square foot of space it can build at the last government-released plot in River Valley Green. That land cost will directly shape what buyers pay when units eventually launch.
items.meaning: The high price paid for this land means future units here are likely to be expensive at launch, so factor that in if you are watching the River Valley area.

items.headline: Bukit Timah maisonette breaks town record at $1.51 million
items.summary: A 38-year-old HDB maisonette in Bukit Timah sold for $1,508,888, the highest price ever recorded for an HDB flat in that area. The large size and address carried the price to that level.
items.meaning: Large, older HDB flats in prime areas are still fetching record prices, giving buyers and sellers a reference point for what the top of the HDB resale market looks like.
